title: VisOnlyQA: Large Vision Language Models Still Struggle with Visual Perception of Geometric Information

task_A_numeric.topic: What does this paper claim about the accuracy gap between state-of-the-art LVLMs and humans on geometric perception tasks?
task_A_numeric.exact_quote: state-of-the-art LVLMs, including GPT-4o and Gemini 2.5 Pro, perform poorly in the basic geometric perception tasks in VisOnlyQA (48.8% and 79.0% in accuracy on the Real split), while human performance is nearly perfect (93.5%).

task_A_prose.topic: What does this paper claim about the role of language models in LVLM geometric perception performance?
task_A_prose.exact_quote: This finding suggests that language models play a crucial role in processing visual information encoded by ViT, and strong language models are needed even in geometric perception tasks that do not involve challenging reasoning or knowledge.

task_B.topic: GPT-4o's accuracy on the VisOnlyQA Real split
task_B.true_claim: GPT-4o achieves 48.8% accuracy on the VisOnlyQA Real split, well below human performance of 93.5%.

task_C.figure: Table 1 (accuracy of 23 VLMs on VisOnlyQA tasks)
task_C.note: repo exists with eval scripts, command not in paper prose
